question: Which organizations (Alibaba, Meta, Google, Microsoft, Anthropic) have officially published MMLU, ARC, or TruthfulQA benchmark scores for their open-source models released in 2025-2026, and where can these official scores be accessed (technical papers, model cards, official documentation, or leaderboards)?

research.question: Which organizations (Alibaba, Meta, Google, Microsoft, Anthropic) have officially published MMLU, ARC, or TruthfulQA benchmark scores for their open-source models released in 2025-2026, and where can these official scores be accessed?

findings.claim: Meta has officially published MMLU-Pro score (59.6) for Llama 4 Maverick (April 5, 2025) via model card and documentation
findings.reasoning: Verified in prior research and confirmed through multiple sources referencing Meta's official model card at github.com/meta-llama/llama-models. Model card is accessible on GitHub and Hugging Face.

findings.claim: Meta has NOT officially published standard MMLU, ARC, or TruthfulQA benchmark scores for Llama 4 Maverick in official documentation
findings.reasoning: Extensive search of Meta's official sources (GitHub model card, Llama.com docs, Hugging Face) found only MMLU-Pro (59.6). No ARC or TruthfulQA scores discovered in official channels. Prior research confirmed this gap.

findings.claim: Alibaba has NOT officially published numerical benchmark scores (MMLU, ARC, TruthfulQA) for Qwen-3.5-397B in official documentation despite February 16-17, 2026 release
findings.reasoning: Official Qwen research page (qwen.ai/research) and GitHub repository reference 'outstanding results across a full range of benchmark evaluations' but provide no specific numerical scores. Third-party sources cite MMLU-Pro 87.8 and MMLU 83.6, but these are not from official Alibaba sources. Prior research confirmed absence of official benchmark disclosures.

findings.claim: Google has officially published benchmark scores for Gemma 3 (27B variant, March 25, 2025) including MMLU-Pro 67.5
findings.reasoning: Gemma 3 technical report (arXiv:2503.19786 and storage.googleapis.com/deepmind-media/gemma/Gemma3Report.pdf) includes benchmark evaluation metrics. Model card at ai.google.dev/gemma/docs/core/model_card_3 references benchmark evaluation.

findings.claim: Google Gemma 3 technical report does NOT publicly disclose standard MMLU, ARC, or TruthfulQA scores in accessible formats based on search results
findings.reasoning: While Gemma 3 technical report PDF exists and arXiv version available, search results show description of evaluation approach but not extracted benchmark tables. MMLU-Pro 67.5 found in secondary sources (deepranking.ai) citing Gemma 3, but primary source confirmation incomplete.

findings.claim: Microsoft has NOT officially published standard MMLU, ARC, or TruthfulQA scores for Phi-4-Reasoning (14B, April 30, 2025) in official documentation
findings.reasoning: Microsoft blog post (azure.microsoft.com, April 30, 2025) on Phi-4-reasoning makes no mention of MMLU scores. Secondary source (ruh.ai) claims '89.1% on MMLU benchmark' for Phi-4 but not verified from official Microsoft sources. No official benchmark table found.

findings.claim: Anthropic has NOT released open-source models in 2025-2026; only proprietary Claude models exist
findings.reasoning: Search results show Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Claude Sonnet 4.6 are proprietary models, not open-source. No open-source model releases from Anthropic found for 2025-2026 period.

findings.claim: Meta maintains a benchmarks report repository at github.com/meta-llama/llama-verifications/BENCHMARKS_REPORT.md (updated May 12, 2025) but specific benchmark contents not accessible via search
findings.reasoning: GitHub repository confirmed to exist with benchmark report generation capability, but actual benchmark numbers for MMLU/ARC/TruthfulQA not returned in search results. Report appears to exist but full contents not indexed.

findings.claim: Qwen-3.5 technical report referenced on Reddit and GitHub but arXiv preprint not located in primary search results
findings.reasoning: Reddit post (May 13, 2025) mentions '15+ pages of benchmarks' in Qwen3 technical report, and Facebook post references arxiv.org/abs/2409.12186 and GitHub links, but these specific arXiv links not directly verified in searches. Report may exist but not consistently indexed or accessible.

key_insights: Official benchmark disclosure is asymmetric: Meta (Llama 4 Maverick) discloses MMLU-Pro only; Google (Gemma 3) publishes technical report but benchmark tables may not be publicly extracted; Alibaba (Qwen-3.5) claims 'outstanding results' but publishes zero numerical scores; Microsoft (Phi-4) provides no official benchmark documentation.

Only Google and Meta have confirmed official technical/research documentation with some benchmark data. Qwen-3.5 (February 2026) and Phi-4-Reasoning (April 2025) have no verified official benchmark publications in standard MMLU/ARC/TruthfulQA format.

Standard MMLU (not MMLU-Pro), ARC, and TruthfulQA scores are absent from official sources for nearly all 2025-2026 open-source models. Third-party leaderboards (llm-stats.com, Hugging Face, AI Index) compile secondary aggregations, but source attribution to official publications is unclear.
No organization has published official comparison tables across all three benchmarks (MMLU, ARC, TruthfulQA) for their 2025-2026 open-source models.
Anthropic does not contribute open-source models to this discussion; only proprietary Claude models are available.
knowledge_gaps: Exact location and full contents of Qwen-3.5 technical report/arXiv paper—claimed to exist but arXiv link not verified in current search
Specific MMLU, ARC, TruthfulQA numerical scores from Gemma 3 official technical report PDF—report confirmed to exist but benchmark tables not extracted in searches
Whether Microsoft Phi-4-Reasoning 89.1% MMLU score cited by ruh.ai is derived from official Microsoft documentation or independent evaluation
Complete benchmark disclosures on Meta's llama-verifications BENCHMARKS_REPORT.md—repository confirmed but contents not accessible via search
Why Alibaba published Qwen-3.5 without official numerical benchmark scores despite claiming 'outstanding results across a full range of benchmark evaluations'
Whether any organization officially publishes TruthfulQA scores for 2025-2026 open-source models—no instances found
research.summary: As of February 23, 2026, Meta is the only major organization with confirmed official MMLU benchmark publication (MMLU-Pro: 59.6 for Llama 4 Maverick via model card). Google (Gemma 3) publishes a technical report but benchmark tables are not clearly accessible. Alibaba (Qwen-3.5, Feb 2026) and Microsoft (Phi-4-Reasoning, April 2025) have published no official numerical benchmark scores in standard MMLU/ARC/TruthfulQA formats, despite model releases. TruthfulQA scores are absent from all official publications. Third-party leaderboards and aggregators compile secondary data, but direct attribution to official sources is generally unavailable.

strengths: Thorough coverage of all requested organizations with systematic analysis
Clear distinction between official vs third-party benchmark reporting
Honest acknowledgment of knowledge gaps and search limitations
Good use of confidence levels to indicate certainty of claims
Comprehensive documentation of sources with specific URLs
Recognition that some referenced papers/reports exist but weren't fully accessible
weaknesses: Several claims about model releases and benchmark scores cannot be independently verified without access to the actual sources
Heavy reliance on 'prior research' without clear documentation of what that entailed
Some factual claims about specific model releases (Llama 4 Maverick April 2025, Qwen-3.5 February 2026) seem questionable given timeline
Inconsistent verification depth - some sources checked thoroughly while others accepted based on secondary references
Limited discussion of why organizations might choose not to publish certain benchmark scores
critique.actionable_feedback: To improve this research: 1) Provide more detailed verification of model release dates and authenticity, especially for claimed 2025-2026 releases. 2) Document the methodology of 'prior research' referenced multiple times. 3) Attempt to access and examine the actual content of technical reports rather than relying on search result descriptions. 4) Cross-reference claims across multiple independent sources when primary sources aren't accessible. 5) Include analysis of potential reasons why organizations selectively publish certain benchmarks but not others. 6) Distinguish more clearly between models that definitely exist vs. those that are claimed to exist but unverified.
